source_note: Community snapshot — not official Capcom data.
research_summary: North American and European community discussions and tier lists from mid-2026 indicate a stable meta with Ed, JP, Mai, and Ryu at the top. Post-EVO 2025 data and the recent Ingrid patch (without major balance changes) have solidified the perceived top tiers, while characters like Jamie and Manon are widely considered underperformers.

tier_notes.ed: Universally considered a top-tier pick due to his powerful and versatile toolset.
tier_notes.jp: Remains a top competitor with strong zoning and mix-ups, performing exceptionally well in tournaments.
tier_notes.mai: A top-tier threat with excellent mid-to-long-range pressure and strong tournament results.
tier_notes.ryu: Has seen a significant rise in popularity and viability, now considered a top-tier character.
tier_notes.akuma: A consistent and powerful character with great flexibility and match-up coverage.
tier_notes.ken: Remains a strong and popular pick in tournaments, though perhaps slightly less dominant than the S-tier.
tier_notes.c-viper: Early community feedback and high-level play suggest she is a very strong addition to the roster.
tier_notes.guile: A patient and fundamental-based zoner that remains a viable and strong pick.
tier_notes.terry: Has proven to be a solid tournament pick with good neutral and damage tools.
tier_notes.rashid: A strong character with good mix-ups and mobility, viable at high levels.
tier_notes.cammy: A consistent presence in top 8s, showing strong tournament viability.
tier_notes.blanka: Won EVO Vegas 2025, proving his strength, though opinions on his consistency vary.
tier_notes.chun-li: A solid, technical character that rewards strong fundamentals.
tier_notes.luke: A historically strong character who remains a potent pick, though slightly outshone by current top tiers.
tier_notes.m-bison: A popular and strong character, with good tournament representation.
tier_notes.sagat: Received a boost from the December patch and is now considered a solid B-tier character.
tier_notes.aki: A strong and viable mid-tier character with a dedicated player base.
tier_notes.dee-jay: A solid character with strong neutral tools, though his tournament consistency is sometimes questioned.
tier_notes.juri: A solid pick with a good neutral game, but often considered to require more effort to win with.
tier_notes.kimberly: Has seen good tournament usage, but her performance is often inconsistent.
tier_notes.zangief: A grappler who can be very strong in the right hands, but has difficult match-ups.
tier_notes.dhalsim: A niche zoner who is extremely difficult to play but can be effective in the right match-ups.
tier_notes.e-honda: A dominant character in lower ranks, but considered to have clear weaknesses at a tournament level.
tier_notes.jamie: Consistently placed in the lowest tier due to his risky drink mechanic and weak early game.
tier_notes.manon: Another character frequently mentioned as being among the weakest in the current meta.
tier_notes.marisa: Considered a situational or low-tier pick, with poor tournament presence.
tier_notes.lily: Generally placed in the lowest tier, struggling against the majority of the roster.
tier_notes.elena: Lacks significant tournament presence and is viewed as a weaker addition.
tier_notes.alex: Recent addition with limited high-level data; early opinions place him in the lower tiers.
tier_notes.ingrid: Newest character; community tier lists still settling. High mobility but currently a wildcard.

controversial.why: Some pros rank him higher due to damage and Kobayan, others low-tier vs zoners.
Split between A-tier after buffs vs solid predictable B-tier.
Many place S-tier; some pros say slightly overrated (A+).

rising_underdogs.why: Huge surge in usage and tournament success.
Kobayan results raised his placement in some lists.

sources_consulted: EventHubs - Evo 2025 stats, Lab Monster top 5, Snake Eyez tier list
TRLST - Post Evo & Viper Release Tier List
MEGASHOCK - Community thread on Jamie, Manon, and Nemo's tier list
Gamer.org - Ingrid patch analysis and meta impact
